steps.title: Simplify the left side
steps.content: $\frac{18x}{9y} = \frac{2x}{y}$, so the equation becomes:$$\frac{2x}{y} = 2\sqrt{w+17}$$Divide both sides by 2:$$\frac{x}{y} = \sqrt{w+17}$$

steps.title: Eliminate the square root by squaring both sides
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $w = \sqrt{\frac{x}{y}} - 17$: takes the square root instead of squaring — reverses the operation. Incorrect.
  • B) $w = \sqrt{\frac{36x}{18y}} - 17$: fails to simplify $\frac{18x}{9y}$ correctly and takes a square root instead of squaring. Incorrect.
  • C) $w = \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2 - 17$: matches the derived result exactly. That matches Choice C — $w={ \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)}^{2}-17$.
  • D) $w = \left(\frac{36x}{18y}\right)^2 - 17$: simplifies $\frac{36x}{18y} = \frac{2x}{y}$, but forgets to divide by 2 before squaring, so $\left(\frac{2x}{y}\right)^2 = \frac{4x^2}{y^2} \neq \left(\frac{x}{y}\right)^2$. Incorrect.
